content_markdown: What is the main role of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse effect?

options.label: A
options.text: It absorbs incoming radiation from the Sun.

options.label: B
options.text: It absorbs outgoing radiation from the Earth.

options.label: C
options.text: It reflects incoming radiation from the Sun.

options.label: D
options.text: It reflects outgoing radiation from the Earth.

vibe_explanation: Greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide are transparent to short-wavelength radiation from the Sun but absorb long-wavelength (infrared) radiation emitted by the Earth's surface. This absorption traps energy in the atmosphere, leading to the greenhouse effect.
